case_title: IN RE: CALIFORNIA STATE LABOR COMMISSIONER
cause_of_action: OTHER CIVIL PETITIONS (petition to compel compliance with investigative subpoena)

PETITION PETITION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH INVESTIGATIVE SUBPOENA FILED BY PETITIONER CALIFORNIA STATE LABOR COMMISSIONER DIVISION OF LABOR STANDARDS ENFORCEMENT, DEPARTMENT OF INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS, STATE OF CALIFORNIA, ON BEHALF OF THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA AS TO RESPONDENT SHEHADEH, AYMAN TALEB AN INDIVIDUAL DOING BUSINESS AS CYBELLE'S PIZZA

LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING RE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY THE INVESTIGATIVE SUBPOENA ISSUED BY THE DLSE SHOULD NOT BE ENFORCED IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SERVE THE MOVING PAPERS AND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE COURT'S ORDER OF FEBRUARY 7, 2017. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189

calendar.matters:
Hearing Re Order To Show Cause Why The Investigative Subpoena Issued By The Dlse Should Not Be Enforced
Off Calendar Mar-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Hearing Re Order To Show Cause Why The Investigative Subpoena Issued By The DLSE Should Not Be Enforced is taken off calendar without prejudice to being refiled. The moving party failed to serve the moving papers and order to show cause in compliance with the court's order of February 7, 2017. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
